data.one_liner: A month of meals, planned to your macros and budget, ordered with one tap.

short_descriptions.app_store_short: Plan a month of meals to your macros and budget. Rasa orders each meal via Swiggy — one tap at a time. Nothing spent silently. Nutrition figures are estimates.
short_descriptions.one_sentence: Rasa plans a month of meals around your macros and your budget, then orders them for you a tap at a time.
short_descriptions.tweet_length: You already order in every day. Rasa plans your month to your macros and your budget, and orders it one tap at a time. No 4pm 'what do I eat.' No blown budget. You always tap yes.

data.long_description:
You already order food delivery most days. Rasa just makes it smart, balanced, and within budget.

Answer a few questions once — your diet, allergens, goals, what you can spend, and what you can't stand — and Rasa builds a 30-day plan that fits your macros and stays under a hard monthly cap. Then, at each mealtime, it checks the restaurant, the price and the fit, and nudges you: 'Grilled chicken bowl, ₹240, arrives ~12:35 — confirm?' You tap yes. It arrives. That's the whole thing.

No daily 15-minute scroll. No 4pm decision drain. No delivery bill you can't account for. No diet that collapses by Wednesday because you couldn't see a dish's macros.

Rasa is built on a few firm promises. Nothing is ordered or spent without your one-tap confirm. Allergens you flag are hard-blocked before anything reaches your cart. A visible ledger shows planned versus actual spend against your cap. And every nutrition figure is a clearly labelled estimate — grounded in an India-native food database, targeted across the whole day. Rasa is about everyday balance, not medical claims.

At launch, Rasa orders through Swiggy, with Zomato, Instamart and tiffin planned next — one plan, one budget, across every food source. It's a health-framed subscription, not a fee on your food: you pay for the planning, the macros and the budget guardrail, from ₹149/month.

Think of Rasa as the competent friend who cooks — the one who texts 'sorted your lunch, tap to confirm.' Not a diet coach yelling macros. Just your meals handled, your numbers roughly on track, your spend under control, and you always holding the tap.

feature_benefit_table.benefit: Your routine meals decided once, so you stop choosing every day.
Food that fits your protein and calorie targets, estimated across the day.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: Figures labelled as estimates; no medical claims (FSSAI-safe).

feature_benefit_table.benefit: Eat out daily and still end the month under the number you set.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: Framed as health/budget guardrail, never a convenience fee.

feature_benefit_table.benefit: A 5-second yes before each order — smart, but never behind your back.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: No silent ordering or spending, ever.

feature_benefit_table.benefit: Flagged allergens are blocked before they can reach your cart.
See planned vs actual spend, per meal, day and month.
Anti-repetition plus ratings that teach Rasa your taste over time.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: Swap any meal in a tap.

feature_benefit_table.benefit: Set up in your own ChatGPT/Claude and paste the result in — zero-friction, shareable.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: Collects goals with no medical claims.

feature_benefit_table.benefit: One budget and plan across Swiggy, Zomato, Instamart and tiffin.
feature_benefit_table.guardrail: Swiggy-only at launch; expands behind one adapter.

who_its_for: Metro tech professionals (26-35) who order in 12-25 times a month and are tired of thinking about it.
Macro-trackers whose meal prep collapses midweek and who want restaurant food that fits their numbers.
who_its_not_for: People wanting fully autonomous ordering with no confirmation (Rasa always asks).
Anyone seeking medical or therapeutic dietary claims (Rasa provides wellness estimates only).
data.compliance_note: Nutrition figures shown in Rasa are wellness estimates, not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Allergen handling depends in part on restaurant-provided data; Rasa surfaces flags clearly and blocks flagged allergens before checkout.
